title: City Council Delays Night Bus Expansion
text: The Harbor City council voted 6-5 to delay a planned night bus expansion after a heated budget hearing. Supporters said the route would help hospital workers and late-shift students, while opponents argued the city should fix existing service gaps before adding new lines. Several residents described missing the last bus after evening classes. The mayor called for a revised plan with a smaller pilot and clearer cost estimates.

title: University Media Lab Opens Community Studio
text: Eastern Bay University opened a community media studio that will allow student groups and neighborhood organizations to record podcasts and short video explainers. Administrators presented the project as an investment in civic participation and digital storytelling. Student organizers welcomed the new space but said long-term access rules will matter more than opening-day speeches.

title: 市政府推出校园周边慢行改造试点
text: 海湾市政府宣布在三所高校周边启动慢行交通改造试点，包括新增斑马线、夜间照明和共享单车停放区。市交通部门表示，方案旨在回应学生晚间出行安全问题，并鼓励低碳通勤。部分商户担心施工会影响门店客流，但学生代表认为，改善步行环境有助于降低事故风险。

title: Food Delivery Platform Faces Worker Safety Questions
text: After two delivery riders were injured during heavy rain, local labor groups criticized the QuickDrop platform for keeping bonus targets unchanged during severe weather alerts. The company said safety reminders were sent through the app and promised to review dispatch timing. Worker advocates argued that reminders are not enough if pay incentives still reward risky deliveries.
